## Purpose and goals

The Topic page acts as a thematic entry point for users interested in a particular subject area. It serves as both a hub for exploration and a summary of the latest and most relevant statistical information available on that topic.

This template aims to:
* Provide users with an accessible snapshot of key statistics
* Help users navigate directly to related data or publications
* Showcase featured content to highlight current or significant releases
* Maintain consistency and clarity across all topic areas

## Key features and structure

The Topic page template is composed of several key sections that work together to support exploration and discovery:

### Page header

The header contains the topic title and an introductory summary. This short paragraph provides context about what the topic covers and what types of content users can expect to find.

### Key facts and figures

This section presents a small number of headline statistics or indicators that represent important aspects of the topic. Each fact or figure can act as a navigational element, linking users directly to the relevant statistical release, dataset, or article.

When used well, these key figures provide users with an instant sense of the scale or significance of a topic while encouraging deeper exploration.

### Featured content

Where appropriate, the page may highlight one or more pieces of featured content. This could be a recent statistical bulletin, data visualisation, or insight report that warrants additional visibility.

Featured content appears near the top of the page to attract user attention and encourage engagement with priority material.

### Related content

This section lists and categorises other relevant resources within the same topic area, such as:
* Statistical articles or bulletins
* Datasets and time series
* Methodology or guidance documents
* Interactive tools, dashboards, or visualisations

Clear categorisation helps users quickly identify the type of content they are looking for and understand how it relates to the wider topic.

### Contact or further information

Optionally, a standardised contact component can be included at the bottom of the page. This should direct users to the relevant statistical team or provide a point of contact for feedback or questions.
